Because I told you about, that if you get it wrong you have to start again. Its an incredibly long and complex process and its just so time-consuming taking it that way. Viv: The problem is Eric, you never have time. A, Im not stupid, I can learn and I have done other finance projects in different institutions, so Im sure theyre not fundamentally different. But whenever I come to you, and I say, Well, let me run with this, you just say, No, no, no, Ill do this one, Ill do it. You wont let go and let me learn and maybe its something to do with your time management and not my time management. George: Given that there are a number of elements that you would both be looking to have in the plan, finance is obviously one of them. If we think about those as being part of the overall plan that we want to see if we can develop today, is it right that we should perhaps take something like finance and perhaps one other as examples of how we might approach everything to do with our projects. All those sorts of issues, is that one way of approaching this that you would find useful, or would you rather do it in a different way? Eric: Well, I can see that is a way forward and certainly that, as I said, that finance project is the one that takes up the most of my time as its the most complex. conversation_text: M. Meyer: Sean, it sounds like you want her to bring the problems to you directly rather than take them to co-workers or up the ladder. Sean McNeil: Yes, shes skipping over me. I mean, how do I know whats bothering her if she doesnt tell me? I cant read minds. Melissa Myer: Okay. Nancy Butler: Well, maybe what we could do is you set the time and then we have 10 minutes where we air our concerns about the other. Ten minutes, twice a month. Sean McNeil: I dont want to feel like were attacking people. What type of day do we do it? I dont want to get all heated up and angry and then have to go back to work. Maybe what we should do is have a drink together. Nancy Butler: I dont want to go out with you. I dont want to have a drink with you. Youre my supervisor. Sean McNeil: Im not dating you. Im trying to find a casual place which is neutral. Nancy Butler: Why dont we meet in the cafeteria and have coffee or lunch? Sean McNeil: It doesnt really take it out of the job. Melissa Myer: Sean, you said you dont want to have to go back to work just in case some hard feelings are brought up or something. Could you meet for 10 minutes in the cafeteria after shift twice a month? Sean McNeil: If its necessary. Melissa Myer: Okay. Sean McNeil: What if we dont have anything to talk about? Melissa Myer: Well, maybe that could be an email. Maybe you set it up in a couple of days in advance: Hey, I dont have anything to talk about this week. Do you want to skip it? You guys could have that conversation before that so youre not wasting that 10 minutes if you dont need to Nancy Butler: Memo? Sean McNeil: Perhaps we could meet in the Personnel Office, where there arent prying ears. Melissa Myer: Sure. Does that sound okay with you? Nancy Butler: I dont want people gossiping about you and I meeting in the Personnel Office because you know how observant people are. I dont want them talking about you and me because we are like oil and vinegar and why we would be meeting in the Personnel Office? Sean McNeil: I dont like us sitting in the cafeteria with ears around. That looks even weirder. More eyes watching us. Nancy Butler: Well, you get here an hour early. Ill come in early. Sean McNeil: Okay. Kelly Tansik: I find that what you guys have mentioned is really good, but its still pretty vague. You mentioned twice a month. Is there a certain? At the beginning of the month? The middle of the month? The middle? The end? Lets just narrow this down so that it is something you can concretely agree to. If you say to meet on Wednesday, on Monday, if you guys are okay, then, you can send each other a message, saying, Okay, we dont have to meet, versus, Oh, I dont know if I was going to send something Nancy Butler: Why dont we do it on the 15th and the 30th of every month? Sean McNeil: We cant do it on the 30th because Ive got a project [sort 1:33:10]. Melissa Myer: What if you picked a specific day of the week? I dont know if you have a less productive day, where youre not doing quite as much one day in manufacturing, or if you want to take the weekend to think over what you want to talk about and cool off and do it on Monday. Sean McNeil: I cant do Mondays. Melissa Myer: You cant do Mondays? Okay. Nancy Butler: Wednesdays are slow, slow days. Why dont we do it on the first and third Monday of the month? Sean McNeil: Ive still got projects coming in on the 1st. Melissa Myer: Not the day, the 1st; the first Monday. Nancy Butler: The first Monday. The first real Monday of the Sean McNeil: You mean the first Wednesday? Melissa Myer: Wednesday, yes. Nancy Butler: Oh, excuse me. Yes, Wednesday. Wednesdays. The first Wednesday during the first week, and then the third week. Sean McNeil: Provided it doesnt fall on the 1st. Nancy Butler: Right.
